manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: pumpkin, pound, tooth, fire, dice, plot, kangaroo, bow, ketchup.
Your opponent's team words are: pistol, fly, soul, heart, space, film, egypt, wake.
Distractor words are: fan, capital, ivory, orange, grass, slip, africa.
The kill words are: antarctica.

message.content:
.CLUE: cooking
TARGETS: pumpkin, ketchup, fire, dice, pound
message.reasoning:
We need to provide a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words. Must not be any of the words in any list or contain parts of them. Also cannot be a kill word. Provide clue and list of target words we want partner to guess, comma-separated.

We need to think of clue that semantically links to multiple team words: pumpkin, pound (as verb or unit), tooth, fire, dice, plot, kangaroo, bow (as weapon or bend), ketchup.

We want to maximize number but must avoid opponent/distractor confusion.

Find a common theme: maybe "red"? Pumpkin is orange, ketchup is red, fire is red, but also "red" not in opponent list. However "red" might be associated with "pistol"? Not strongly. But "red" could be associated with "fly"? Not too. Could be safe.

But clue must not be part of any word: "red" not part of any.

What target words relate to "red"? Pumpkin (orange, not red). Ketchup (red). Fire (red). Bow (could be red bow). But also "dice" could be red dice, "pound" could be British pound (red note). Not ideal.

Maybe "bunny"? Has kangaroo, but not others.

Maybe "bushfire"? It's fire.

Better clue: "Australian". Relates to kangaroo, maybe "bow"? Not.

Maybe "cooking". Relates to pumpkin (used in cooking), ketchup (condiment), fire (cooking), dice (to dice vegetables), bow (bow tie?), pound (pound meat). Tooth? Not.

But "cooking" could lead to guess many.

But need to ensure no opponent words strongly associated: "fire" also could be "space" (fire in space?). "film" (cooking film?). "pistol"? Not.

Maybe clue "baked". Pumpkin baked, ketchup baked? Not.

Maybe "sauce". Ketchup is sauce, maybe "pound" sauce? Not.

Maybe clue "game". Dice, plot (like storyline game), bow (archery), pound (pound as weight in games), fire (fire in video games), kangaroo (jump). Might be too broad may lead to opponent words "film" (games). But okay.

We want a clue linking to at least 3-4 team words without leading to opponent. Choose "archery"? That links to bow, maybe pound (pound as poundage of a bow), maybe kangaroo (hopping arrow)? Not.

Maybe "Halloween". Relates to pumpkin, bow? (bow? maybe bow tie). Fire (jack-o-lantern). Dice? Not.

Maybe "crush". Pound (to crush), ketchup (crushed tomatoes), tooth (crush?), fire (crush?). Not.

Maybe "seed". Pumpkin seeds, dice seeds? Not.

Better: "Mouth". Tooth, ketchup (eaten), fire (burn), pound (eat?), bow (bow as in bowing? maybe not). Not.

Clue "Red" we can target pumpkin? Not great.

Clue "Spice". Fire (spicy), ketchup (spicy), maybe dice (spice dice?). Not.

Perhaps we can give multiple clues across turns. But for now one clue.

Pick "cooking" targeting: pumpkin, ketchup, fire, dice (to dice), pound (to pound meat). That's five. Does "cooking" conflict with opponent words? "fly", "soul", "heart", "space", "film", "egypt", "wake". Not strongly. Distractors: fan, capital, ivory, orange, grass, slip, africa. "cooking" not.

Thus:

CLUE: cooking
TARGETS: pumpkin, ketchup, fire, dice, pound

That seems okay. Ensure clue not part of any word: "cooking" not part of others. Good.

Return in format
